name: Marmelete Geology Route
name_original: De Marmelete ao Miradouro dos Picos — percurso circular
description: This moderate 3.8-mile (6.1 km) circular jogging route from Marmelete offers panoramic views from Miradouro dos Picos.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Marmelete are set within the mountainous Serra de Monchique, offering a diverse terrain of rolling hills and higher peaks. The region is characterized by extensive cork oak and eucalyptus forests, providing shaded paths and natural scents. Trails often follow verdant landscapes, including features like Barranco do Passil. This natural setting provides a range of inclines and declines for dynamic running experiences. Best jogging routes around Marmelete The most popular jogging route is Marmelete Geology Route, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes about 43 minutes to complete, exploring the geological features of the area. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Algarve International Circuit, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path, offering views of the surrounding landscape near the circuit. Local runners also love the The Planalto Snack Bar – Foia Summit loop from Monchique, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through the Monchique hills towards Foia Summit, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Marmelete is defined by rolling hills, cork oak forests, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for various ability levels, from gentle paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Marmelete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Marmelete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Marmelete?
A: Marmelete offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 70 trails available for you to explore. These range from easy paths to more challenging ascents, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Marmelete has several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or families. The region features gentle paths through verdant landscapes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5285683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Algarve International Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.4-mile path that offers scenic views without extreme difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Marmelete?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Marmelete are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6873923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Planalto Snack Bar – Foia Summit loop from Monchique&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 4.5-mile circular trail that takes you through the Monchique hills.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I experience on the jogging trails?
A: Jogging routes around Marmelete offer diverse landscapes within the Serra de Monchique. You'll encounter rolling hills, extensive cork oak and eucalyptus forests providing shaded paths, and verdant riparian vegetation, particularly along features like Barranco do Passil. The region is known as the 'garden of the Algarve' for its lush scenery.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the running paths?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Serra de Monchique provides numerous panoramic viewpoints. Trails often lead to high points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/735919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foia Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Algarve, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/111049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picota Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views across the Algarve coast and beyond. The Marmelete Walking Trail (PR6 MCQ) also provides panoramic views over the Algarve coast from Miradouro dos Picos.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Marmelete dog-friendly?
A: Many of the trails in the Marmelete area, especially those through the cork oak and eucalyptus forests, are suitable for jogging with dogs. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while running?
A: While running in Marmelete, you can encounter several natural attractions. You might pass through a beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1034710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cork oak grove in Monchique&lt;/a&gt;, or even spot a significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5694823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cork oak in Monchique&lt;/a&gt;. The Cascata do Barbelote Trail leads to a picturesque waterfall, offering a serene experience amidst lush greenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Marmelete?
A: The running routes in Marmelete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the tranquility of the cork oak and eucalyptus forests, and the inspiring panoramic views from the higher peaks.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Marmelete offers more difficult routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21821192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foia Summit – Fóia agricultural terraces loop from Monchique&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.6-mile (18.7 km) trail with over 650 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding workout and stunning vistas.

Q: Can I connect to long-distance trails from Marmelete?
A: Yes, Marmelete is connected to the Via Algarviana (GR 13), a long-distance hiking trail that traverses hidden corners of the south of Portugal. Sections of this trail are suitable for trail running, offering varied experiences through rural landscapes and the opportunity to explore further afield.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the geological features of the area?
A: Indeed, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420548304/marmelete-geology-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmelete Geology Route&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.8-mile (6.1 km) trail specifically designed to explore the unique geological features of the region, providing an interesting and informative running experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Marmelete?
A: The Serra de Monchique region, including Marmelete, offers pleasant conditions for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and lush scenery. Even in summer, the extensive cork oak and eucalyptus forests offer shaded paths, making it possible to run comfortably, especially during the cooler morning or late afternoon hours.
tips: Marmelete is a parish in the Algarve region, Portugal, part of the municipality of Monchique. The village stands out for its historic cork production, which was, and still is, an important part of the local economy.
After crossing the trails through the eucalyptus forest, you arrive at Miradour dos Picos. The site is located at 565m above sea level. Take the opportunity to stop and enjoy the panoramic view.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the From Marmelete to Miradouro dos Picos — circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is designed for jogging and typically takes around 43 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The 6.11 km version of this circular route is generally considered an easy hike, suitable for most fitness levels. While komoot classifies it as 'moderate' for jogging, the paths are mostly accessible. There is a specific 1 km climb to Miradouro dos Picos with an average gradient of 11%, which might be challenging for absolute beginners but is manageable.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near the Marmelete Church and continues along Rua Francisco Furtado in Marmelete Village. Parking is generally available within the village, close to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter asphalt sections (around 3.69 km), paved paths (around 1.96 km), and natural trails (approximately 433 m). It passes through eucalyptus forests, cork oak trees, and traditional agricultural terraces, requiring sure-footedness in some parts.

faqs.question: What can I see along the From Marmelete to Miradouro dos Picos — circular route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience scenic mountain landscapes and pass through Marmelete Village. A major highlight is the Miradouro dos Picos, offering magnificent panoramic views over the Algarve coastline. You'll also see diverse vegetation like gum rockrose, heather, strawberry trees, and cork oaks, and pass by the historic Chapel of Santo António.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this route align with other official trails. The entire route is covered by the Marmelete (Rota da Geologia), and it also shares sections with the Percurso Pedestre de Marmelete (74% of the route) and the Via Algarviana (69% of the route).

faqs.question: Is the From Marmelete to Miradouro dos Picos — circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas where you might encounter wildlife or other hikers. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the From Marmelete to Miradouro dos Picos — circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Monchique mountains, including this route, are pleasant for jogging during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring more rain, though the Algarve generally enjoys mild winters.

faqs.question: Are there any local amenities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Marmelete Village is known for its local gastronomy. You can find honey, traditional sweets, and *medronho* (arbutus-berry brandy). You might also visit the Casa do Medronho to learn about its production. There are cafes and small eateries within the village.

faqs.question: Are there any temporary closures or important notices for this trail?
faqs.answer: As of December 3, 2025, the PR6 MCQ - Marmelete Walking Trail (which incorporates part of this route) was temporarily closed due to forestry work and tree felling. It is highly advisable to check for current updates on local tourism websites, such as visitmonchique.pt, before planning your trip.
